storyInfo: Outside the First Temple reconstruction site, the ferryman Xanthos targets Titi and the relic of Athenius that she has brought.

attributes.content: 'And so Kereseira, the singer of epics, the gentlest hero of Agamem, hung her harp upon the branches of the laurel tree—'
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: 'Its body made from the tallest firs atop Mount Hymnoi, its strings made from the bravest burdenbeast that roamed the Agnes Plains;'
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: 'When it played, all things in the Land of Heroes sang together, riding upon the winds in freedom.'
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: No wonder you came up with the idea of holding the exchange ceremony on the temple square when you saw the completed statue of Kereseira.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: You brought Kereseira's true harp, the one that used to be displayed in the First Temple of Old Athenius?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Yes!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Legend has it that Kereseira was inspired to carve a massive harp from mountain rocks and firs after hearing the mountains tell their stories beneath Mt. Hymnoi.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: It was a blessed harp that only sang on important occasions, or when played by a great hero like Kereseira, offering a song that touches the people's hearts.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: The First Temple was destroyed when the Sargonians invaded Old Athenius.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Grieving the tragedy, the harp sang no more, and afterwards went missing.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Until Padishah Khery found it in his collection.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I don't know how it ended up in Sargon—perhaps a Sargonian soldier looted it from Old Athenius and sold it to the padishah's ancestor.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I don't think anyone alive in Athenius has ever seen the original harp.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: The only people who would know anything about the harp are a few priests well-versed with ancient texts, or oribenders like Kassandra who directly communicate with the heroes.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Does it still make a sound?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Yes.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: We performed an inspection on the strings. They're a little aged, but still in playing condition.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I see. Perhaps the silence of the harp was simply a metaphor, as is often the case in Minoan stories...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Where are the ferrymen?
attributes.name: Innkeeper

attributes.content: We pay these outrageous rents to be by the canal and get fresh fins!
attributes.name: Innkeeper

attributes.content: The goods are here, keeper.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Finally! Any more delays, and we won't be ready for the diplomatic delegation!
attributes.name: Innkeeper

attributes.content: Should I unload the goods for you?
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Hey, why is the boat docked so far today? People will be falling into the water trying to unload stuff.
attributes.name: Innkeeper

attributes.content: Bring the boat closer, please. As close to the kitchen as you can.
attributes.name: Innkeeper

attributes.content: Okay.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Thank you. Why are you the one delivering today, by the way? It's usually Xanthos who does it.
attributes.name: Innkeeper

attributes.content: I apologize. Master Xanthos had business.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Well, it's no problem. As long as the food gets here and the kitchen can get to work.
attributes.name: Innkeeper

attributes.content: You've been with Xanthos for a few years, haven't you? He must have his reasons for planning it this way. Not concerned at all.
attributes.name: Innkeeper

attributes.content: Sorry, Pallas. Everyone in the temple has their hands full today. I simply can't free up anyone from the moving crew.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I wanted to get the harp into the First Temple ahead of time to see if how it fits Kereseira's statue.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I should have had someone big and strong to help with carrying the harp, though. It's just the two of us...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Kereseira was not particularly tall according to historical records. People took particular note of the contrast when she stood next to her harp.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Maybe I could even do it myself—
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Oof... it's heavy. Is this box full of lead or something?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Well, we entered Athenius by the ancient route, which is pretty rough. We couldn't risk any damage to the relic.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I had the museum staff prepare a packaging that's fireproof, waterproof, shockproof, and impact-proof, which unfortunately added a ton of weight to it.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: On the other hand, it wouldn't get a scratch on it, even if an earthquake suddenly shook a whole house down onto it!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I can see you take great care of your museum and collection.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: I went to Columbia to study museology because I was curious about everything, and ended up working in a museum when I went back to Sargon.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: But my father died young, and my family's a far cry from its past glory... honestly, I'm not sure why the padishah picked me to lead the diplomatic mission...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: But it's my job now, and I want to do it right!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Did I ever tell you that you—
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: What happened?! Everything okay, Titi?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Don't worry, the harp's unharmed!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I was asking about YOU!
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Speaking of earthquakes... good thing the relic is well-protected.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: What's all the commotion downstairs?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Looks like a boat rammed straight into the hotel kitchen.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: It's over... it's all over...
attributes.name: Lamenting Male Voice

attributes.content: What kind of a ferryman are you?! Full sails from five meters away? On a windy day like this? You knew your boat would go even faster than Hermes the Messenger!
attributes.name: Lamenting Male Voice

attributes.content: You wanted the boat as close to the kitchen as possible.
attributes.name: Calm Male Voice

attributes.content: I didn't mean INSIDE the kitchen!
attributes.name: Lamenting Male Voice

attributes.content: Make way, make way.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Peace officer's already dispersing onlookers. Don't crowd around like this. I need to see what happened.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Lykeion?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: You'll uphold justice, won't you, Grammateús?
attributes.name: Innkeeper

attributes.content: I just wanted the boat bringing the ingredients to be closer to the kitchen, but this kid rammed it right into the place!
attributes.name: Innkeeper

attributes.content: I'm sorry. I... don't know how to operate a boat.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Who do you think you're fooling? You've been studying with Xanthos at the crossing every day for years!
attributes.name: Innkeeper

attributes.content: Theoretical knowledge does not equal practical skill.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Why you—!
attributes.name: Innkeeper

attributes.content: Enough!
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: I can testify that this man never passed a single chariot racing test back in priest school.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Doesn't explain how he didn't see this huge stop sign, does it? The boat ran right through the fence!
attributes.name: Innkeeper

attributes.content: Didn't see...?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: (Has Oripathy spread to his perceptual system? Is that why Lena made a home visit the other day?)
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Lykeion...
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Why do you look like you think I'm going to die soon—
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: No, no, no! Don't say that! Even if it's true...
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Excuse me, Grammateús. I know the two of you used to be classmates, but you're here as an official, and my kitchen has suffered significant damage.
attributes.name: Innkeeper

attributes.content: Here.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: This is... your coin purse?
attributes.name: Innkeeper

attributes.content: I'll pay for the damage. Take what you need.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Who's telling you to pay for me?
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: You never got a proper job since you left priest school. I know Xanthos takes care of your room and board, but I doubt you have much money saved.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Don't worry about it. Spend your money on treatment. Don't forget you still owe me a debate.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: I'm fine, really... *cough* *cough*
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: The coughing... an acute attack? Doctor! I need a doctor over here!
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: It's just the smoke, you idiot! Go get someone to fight the fire, Grammateús!
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: The fire is getting stronger. Looks like the boat hit the kitchen stove... *cough* What's with all the smoke?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: We need to get out, get away from the smoke. But the harp...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: The harp's well-protected. I see the officers are already organizing people to fight the fire. It should be under control soon.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Besides, the stairs are too narrow. The harp would get in the way of other people evacuating. Besides... *cough*—
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Are you alright, Pallas?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: You're right. We need to leave. We can come back when the smoke's gone. Let's go.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: A fire? Oh no, oh no! My luggage is still in my room!
attributes.name: Panicking Guest

attributes.content: Don't worry, the fire's in the first-floor kitchen. It's not going to spread to the guestrooms upstairs. Just wait for them to put out the fire and the smoke to disperse.
attributes.name: Peace Officer

attributes.content: Stop crowding and clear the area around the door! There are still people coming down from upstairs—
attributes.name: Peace Officer

attributes.content: Sir! Now's not the time to go inside!
attributes.name: Peace Officer

attributes.content: Feeling better, Pallas?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I'm fine. I just inhaled some smoke.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: We should be good to go back to the room in ten minutes. I'll help you take the harp to the First Temple, just in case anything else happens.
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Thanks.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Oh, sorry. It's so crowded here.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Hey, Pallas, did that guy look familiar to you?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Who?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Did you hear? That kid at the crossing was the one who rammed the boat into the kitchen. He usually looks pretty smart, but I guess he's as dumb as it gets when it comes to piloting a boat.
attributes.name: Gossiper

attributes.content: Where was Xanthos? He's the one who usually delivers the goods.
attributes.name: Bored Listener

attributes.content: Too busy, maybe? But it's not like him to let an inexperienced apprentice get into trouble on his own.
attributes.name: Gossiper

attributes.content: True.
attributes.name: Bored Listener

attributes.content: ...I remember now. It was Xanthos, the ferryman, who ran into me just now.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Xanthos? Are you sure?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: If he had the time to come to the hotel, why would he let an inexperienced apprentice steer the boat and cause an accident? Wait a second...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: I have a bad feeling about this.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: As do I.
attributes.name: Pallas
